name: Peanut Butter Banana Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease and flour a 13x9 inch pan.
2. In a large bowl, mash the bananas and mix with softened margarine until well combined. Stir in creamy peanut butter, then add whipping cream and eggs. Beat until smooth.
3. Add cake mix and all-purpose flour to the batter, mixing on low speed until just moistened. Mix on high speed for 2 minutes.
4. Pour batter into prepared pan and spread evenly.
5. Bake for 45-55 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean. Allow cooling for 15 minutes before removing from the pan.
6. Frosting: In a small bowl, combine cake frosting with banana extract (or vanilla) and yellow food coloring. Stir until smooth.
7. Frost the cooled cake. Drizzle glaze over the frosting by stirring powdered sugar, creamy peanut butter, milk, and vanilla in another small bowl until smooth.
8. Garnish with thinly sliced bananas just before serving. Store leftovers in the refrigerator. (Add 1/2 cup flour to the cake mix if baking at an altitude above 3,500 feet.)
